title: Introduction to Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
text: Standard cost-effectiveness analysis (CEA) answers one question: intervention generate health per pound spent alternatives? aggregates health across patients QALY gained deprived person equals QALY gained least deprived. approach silent benefits — therefore interventions affect health inequalities socioeconomic groups.

title: Introduction to Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
text: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis (DCEA), developed Cookson, Griffin, Norheim Culyer (2020), extends standard CEA : Distributing aggregate health gains across socioeconomic groups. Measuring impact health inequality (SII, Atkinson index, etc.). Evaluating social welfare gain using inequality-aversion weights. Visualising equity-efficiency trade-impact plane.

what: When to use aggregate vs full-form DCEA
title: Introduction to Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis
text: NICE (2025) recommends aggregate DCEA default supplementary analysis technology appraisals equity relevant.

title: Inequality Measurement in DCEA
text: dceasimR provides five inequality measures commonly used DCEA: SII, RII, concentration index, Atkinson index, Gini coefficient.
code: df <- tibble::tibble(   group      = 1:5,   mean_hale  = c(52.1, 56.3, 59.8, 63.2, 66.8),   pop_share  = rep(0.2, 5) )

title: Inequality Measurement in DCEA
text: SII estimates absolute health difference least deprived using weighted regression ridit scores. positive SII means better health advantaged groups.
code: calc_sii(df, "mean_hale", "group", "pop_share") #> $sii #> [1] 18.15 #>  #> $rii #> [1] 0.304326 #>  #> $se_sii #> [1] 0.4112988 #>  #> $p_value #> [1] 2.561597e-05 #>  #> $model #>  #> Call: #> stats::lm(formula = h ~ ridit, weights = w) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        ridit   #>       50.56        18.15

title: Inequality Measurement in DCEA
text: RII expresses SII relative mean health, facilitating comparisons across populations time.
code: calc_rii(df, "mean_hale", "group", "pop_share") #> $sii #> [1] 18.15 #>  #> $rii #> [1] 0.304326 #>  #> $se_sii #> [1] 0.4112988 #>  #> $p_value #> [1] 2.561597e-05 #>  #> $model #>  #> Call: #> stats::lm(formula = h ~ ridit, weights = w) #>  #> Coefficients: #> (Intercept)        ridit   #>       50.56        18.15   #>  #>  #> $se_rii #> [1] 0.006896357

title: Inequality Measurement in DCEA

title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
text: Atkinson SWF evaluates population health penalising inequality according parameter eta (η). Higher η = stronger aversion inequality. Equally Distributed Equivalent (EDE) health key output: level health , equally distributed, give social welfare actual distribution.

what: Choosing eta: evidence from the UK
title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
text: Robson et al. (2017) elicited public preferences health inequality aversion England using questionnaire. central estimate η ≈ 1, range 0.1 4.8 across sample. NICE (2025) mandate specific η expects sensitivity analysis across range including 0, 1, higher values.

title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
code: health  <- c(52.1, 56.3, 59.8, 63.2, 66.8) weights <- rep(0.2, 5)  # eta = 0: no inequality aversion (arithmetic mean) calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 0) #> [1] 59.64  # eta = 1: moderate aversion (geometric mean) calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 1) #> [1] 59.41665  # eta = 5: strong aversion calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 5) #> [1] 58.51938

title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
code: profile <- calc_ede_profile(health, weights, eta_range = seq(0, 10, 0.1)) library(ggplot2) ggplot(profile, aes(eta, ede)) +   geom_line(colour = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +   labs(x = expression(eta), y = "EDE (years)",        title = "EDE Profile") +   theme_minimal()

title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
code: ew <- calc_equity_weights(health, weights, eta = 1) ew  # Q1 (most deprived) gets highest weight #> [1] 1.1361198 1.0513649 0.9898301 0.9365798 0.8861054

title: Social Welfare Functions in DCEA
code: post_health <- health + c(0.5, 0.6, 0.5, 0.4, 0.3) calc_social_welfare(health, post_health, weights, eta = 1) #> $ede_baseline #> [1] 59.41665 #>  #> $ede_post #> [1] 59.88513 #>  #> $delta_ede #> [1] 0.4684784 #>  #> $efficiency_component #> [1] 0.46 #>  #> $equity_component #> [1] 0.008478412

title: NICE Submission Workflow
text: NICE (2025) Methods Support Document (PMG36) health inequalities requires manufacturers submit DCEA supplementary evidence : intervention targets condition documented SES gradients. ICER £20,000/QALY equity may affect decision. committee requests equity evidence.

title: NICE Submission Workflow
text: Use aggregate DCEA unless subgroup-specific evidence available.

what: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
title: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
text: first comprehensive R package Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis (DCEA) — implementing methods endorsed NICE (2025) described Cookson et al. (2020).

title: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
text: Standard cost-effectiveness analysis treats QALY gained deprived patient equivalent QALY gained least deprived. DCEA breaks assumption distributing health gains across socioeconomic groups, measuring inequality impact, applying social welfare function weights. dceasimR provides: Aggregate DCEA (Love-Koh et al. 2019) — NICE-endorsed default method Full-form DCEA — subgroup-specific evidence Inequality indices — SII, RII, concentration index, Atkinson, Gini Social welfare functions — EDE health, equity weights, welfare decomposition Visualisation — equity-efficiency impact plane, Lorenz curves, EDE profiles Preloaded data — England (IMD quintile), Canada (income quintile), regions NICE-formatted export — tables, Excel workbooks, HTML reports Interactive Shiny app — launch_dcea_app()

title: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
text: dceasimR implements DCEA methods described NICE’s modular update PMG36 (Technology Evaluation Methods: Health Inequalities, 2025). aggregate DCEA approach follows Love-Koh et al. (2019) Value Health.

title: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
code: # CRAN (once available) install.packages("dceasimR")  # Development version from GitHub # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("heorlytics/dceasimR")

title: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment
code: library(dceasimR)  # Run aggregate DCEA for a hypothetical NSCLC treatment (NICE TA style) result <- run_aggregate_dcea(   icer            = 28000,   inc_qaly        = 0.45,   inc_cost        = 12600,   population_size = 12000,   disease_icd     = "C34",   # Lung cancer -> uses internal HES utilisation data   wtp             = 20000,   opportunity_cost_threshold = 13000 )  # View summary summary(result)  # Plot equity-efficiency impact plane plot_equity_impact_plane(result)  # Sensitivity over inequality aversion plot_ede_profile(result, eta_range = seq(0, 10, 0.1))  # Export NICE-formatted table generate_nice_table(result, format = "flextable")  # Launch interactive Shiny app launch_dcea_app()

what: Calculate Atkinson Index of Health Inequality — calc_atkinson_index
title: Calculate Atkinson Index of Health Inequality — calc_atkinson_index
text: Atkinson index measures extent inequality health distribution, explicitly incorporating parameter \(\varepsilon\) representing inequality aversion. Higher \(\varepsilon\) values give weight health differences bottom distribution.

text: health_dist Numeric vector health values across population groups. pop_weights Numeric vector population weights (need sum 1; normalised internally). epsilon Inequality aversion parameter (default = 1). Must non-negative. \(\varepsilon = 0\), returns 0 (aversion).

title: Calculate Atkinson Index of Health Inequality — calc_atkinson_index
text: Atkinson index value [0, 1]. value 0 indicates perfect   equality; value approaching 1 indicates maximum inequality.

title: Calculate Atkinson Index of Health Inequality — calc_atkinson_index
text: Atkinson AB (1970). Measurement Inequality. Journal   Economic Theory 2(3): 244-263. doi:10.1016/0022-0531(70)90039-6

NULL, computed group_var weight_var using ridit scoring. type Concentration index variant: "standard" (Kakwani), "erreygers" (bounded), "wagstaff" (normalised).

what: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
text: Uses Atkinson social welfare function calculate EDE health — level health , equally distributed, generate social welfare actual distribution given inequality aversion parameter \(\eta\).

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
code: calc_ede(health_dist, pop_weights, eta = 1)

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
text: health_dist Numeric vector health values group (must strictly positive). pop_weights Numeric vector population weights (normalised sum 1). eta Inequality aversion parameter (numeric scalar, default = 1). \(\eta = 0\): returns arithmetic mean (inequality aversion). \(\eta = 1\): returns geometric mean (moderate aversion). \(\eta > 1\): increasing inequality aversion. NICE relevant range: 0 10.

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
text: EDE health value (numeric scalar). Returns NA   warning health values non-positive.

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
text: $$   \text{EDE}(\eta) =   \left(\sum_i w_i h_i^{1-\eta} \big/ \sum_i w_i\right)^{1/(1-\eta)}   \quad \text{} \eta \neq 1 $$ $$   \text{EDE}(1) = \exp\!\left(\sum_i w_i \ln(h_i)\right)   \quad \text{(geometric mean, } \eta = 1\text{)} $$

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
text: Atkinson AB (1970). Measurement Inequality. Journal   Economic Theory 2(3): 244-263. doi:10.1016/0022-0531(70)90039-6

title: Calculate Equally Distributed Equivalent Health (EDE) — calc_ede
code: health  <- c(60, 63, 66, 69, 72) weights <- rep(0.2, 5)  # eta = 0: arithmetic mean calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 0) #> [1] 66  # eta = 1: geometric mean calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 1) #> [1] 65.8633  # eta = 5: high inequality aversion calc_ede(health, weights, eta = 5) #> [1] 65.31903

what: dceasimR: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment — dceasimR-package
title: dceasimR: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment — dceasimR-package
text: Implements distributional cost-effectiveness analysis (DCEA) described Cookson et al. (2020) methods endorsed NICE (2025). Provides functions aggregate full-form DCEA, inequality measurement, social welfare function evaluation, equity-efficiency impact plane visualisation, sensitivity analysis.

title: dceasimR: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment — dceasimR-package
text: run_aggregate_dcea — Aggregate DCEA (Love-Koh 2019) run_full_dcea — Full-form DCEA subgroup data calc_sii / calc_rii — Inequality indices calc_ede — Equally Distributed Equivalent health plot_equity_impact_plane — Equity-efficiency impact plane get_baseline_health — Preloaded baseline distributions launch_dcea_app — Interactive Shiny application

title: dceasimR: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment — dceasimR-package
text: Cookson R, Griffin S, Norheim , Culyer AJ (2020). Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis. Oxford University Press. Oxford University Press (ISBN:9780198838197). Love-Koh J, Asaria M, Cookson R, Griffin S (2019). Social Distribution Health: Estimating Quality-Adjusted Life Expectancy England. Value Health 22(5): 518-526. doi:10.1016/j.jval.2018.10.007 Asaria M, Griffin S, Cookson R (2016). Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis: Tutorial. Medical Decision Making 36(1): 8-19. doi:10.1177/0272989X15583266

title: dceasimR: Distributional Cost-Effectiveness Analysis for Health Technology Assessment — dceasimR-package
text: Maintainer: Shubhram Pandey shubhram.pandey@heorlytics.com (ORCID)

title: Plot Equity-Efficiency Impact Plane — plot_equity_impact_plane
text: Creates equity-efficiency impact plane described Cookson et al. (2017) Value Health. x-axis shows health inequality impact (change chosen inequality index), y-axis shows net health benefit (efficiency). Four quadrants represent: Win-Win (NE), equity gain efficiency loss (NW), equity loss efficiency gain (SE), Lose-Lose (SW).

title: Plot Equity-Efficiency Impact Plane — plot_equity_impact_plane
code: plot_equity_impact_plane(   dcea_result,   comparators = NULL,   x_axis = "sii_change",   y_axis = "nhb",   show_psa_cloud = TRUE,   show_quadrant_labels = TRUE,   show_threshold_lines = TRUE,   point_labels = NULL,   colour_palette = NULL,   theme_style = "publication" )

title: Plot Equity-Efficiency Impact Plane — plot_equity_impact_plane
text: dcea_result Object class "aggregate_dcea" "full_dcea". comparators Optional list additional DCEA result objects overlay plane (multi-comparator plots). x_axis Inequality metric x-axis. One "sii_change" (default), "atkinson_change", "gini_change". y_axis Health outcome y-axis. One "nhb" (default), "net_monetary_benefit". show_psa_cloud Logical. Show probabilistic scatter cloud PSA data available (default: TRUE). show_quadrant_labels Logical (default: TRUE). show_threshold_lines Logical. Show NHB = 0 inequality = 0 reference lines (default: TRUE). point_labels Optional character vector labels points. colour_palette Optional named character vector hex colours. theme_style Visual theme: "publication" (default) "ggplot_default".

title: Plot Equity-Efficiency Impact Plane — plot_equity_impact_plane
text: ggplot2 object.

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
text: Implements aggregate DCEA approach Love-Koh et al. (2019) Value Health. Uses disease-level healthcare utilisation patterns distribute average health benefits standard CEA across socioeconomic groups. method supported NICE (2025) supplementary analysis technology appraisals.

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
code: run_aggregate_dcea(   icer,   inc_qaly,   inc_cost,   population_size,   wtp = 20000,   disease_icd = NULL,   subgroup_distribution = NULL,   baseline_health = NULL,   equity_var = "imd_quintile",   wtp_for_equity = NULL,   opportunity_cost_threshold = 13000,   psa_results = NULL )

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
text: icer Incremental cost-effectiveness ratio (GBP per QALY). inc_qaly Incremental QALYs per patient (base-case CEA). inc_cost Incremental cost per patient (base-case CEA). population_size Total eligible population size (integer). wtp Willingness--pay threshold GBP/QALY (default: 20000). disease_icd ICD-10 code description HES utilisation lookup. Used distribute benefits across IMD groups subgroup_distribution NULL. Example: "C34" lung cancer. subgroup_distribution Optional named numeric vector (length = number equity subgroups) giving proportion patients group. Names match group labels baseline dataset. Must sum 1. NULL, derived disease_icd via internal lookup. baseline_health Optional tibble get_baseline_health. NULL, uses England IMD quintile data. equity_var Equity stratification variable (default: "imd_quintile"). wtp_for_equity Optional second WTP threshold equity-weighted analysis. opportunity_cost_threshold Cost per QALY care displaced intervention's budget impact (default: 13000, .e., NICE's k threshold). psa_results Optional data frame PSA iteration results (one row per iteration, columns inc_qaly inc_cost).

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
text: object class "aggregate_dcea", named list : summary Key scalar DCEA outputs. by_group Per-group tibble: health gain, opportunity cost,       NHB. inequality_impact Pre/post inequality indices. social_welfare Social welfare results eta. equity_plane_data Data frame plot_equity_impact_plane. metadata Inputs assumptions.

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
text: Love-Koh J, Asaria M, Cookson R, Griffin S (2019). Social   Distribution Health: Estimating Quality-Adjusted Life Expectancy   England. Value Health 22(5): 518-526.   doi:10.1016/j.jval.2018.10.007

title: Run Aggregate DCEA — run_aggregate_dcea
code: result <- run_aggregate_dcea(   icer            = 25000,   inc_qaly        = 0.5,   inc_cost        = 12500,   population_size = 10000,   wtp             = 20000 ) summary(result) #> == Aggregate DCEA Result == #>   ICER:             £25,000 / QALY #>   Incremental QALY: 0.5000 #>   Incremental cost: £12,500 #>   Population size:  10,000 #>   Net Health Benefit: -4615.38 QALYs #>   SII change:         -0.0000 #>   Decision:           Trade-off: equity gain, efficiency loss #>  #> -- Per-group results -- #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   group_label         baseline_hale post_hale   nhb #>   <chr>                       <dbl>     <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 Q1 (most deprived)           52.1      52.0 -923. #> 2 Q2                           56.3      56.2 -923. #> 3 Q3                           59.8      59.7 -923. #> 4 Q4                           63.2      63.1 -923. #> 5 Q5 (least deprived)          66.8      66.7 -923. #>  #> -- Inequality impact -- #> # A tibble: 4 × 5 #>   index           pre     post    change pct_change #>   <chr>         <dbl>    <dbl>     <dbl>      <dbl> #> 1 sii        18.2     18.1     -4.97e-14  -2.74e-13 #> 2 rii         0.304    0.305    4.72e- 4   1.55e- 1 #> 3 gini        0.0487   0.0488   7.55e- 5   1.55e- 1 #> 4 atkinson_1  0.00374  0.00376  1.17e- 5   3.12e- 1

title: Run DCEA Sensitivity Analysis — run_dcea_sensitivity
text: Performs systematic one-way multi-way sensitivity analysis across key DCEA parameters: inequality aversion (\(\eta\)), WTP threshold, opportunity cost threshold, subgroup distribution assumptions, equity measure choice.

title: Run DCEA Sensitivity Analysis — run_dcea_sensitivity
code: run_dcea_sensitivity(   dcea_result,   params_to_vary = "all",   eta_range = 0:10,   wtp_range = NULL,   occ_range = NULL )

title: Run DCEA Sensitivity Analysis — run_dcea_sensitivity
text: dcea_result Object class "aggregate_dcea" "full_dcea". params_to_vary Character vector parameter names vary. Options: "eta", "wtp", "occ_threshold", "subgroup_distribution", "equity_measure", "" (default: ""). eta_range Numeric vector \(\eta\) values eta sensitivity (default: 0:10). wtp_range Numeric vector WTP values test (default: varies ±50% around base case). occ_range Numeric vector opportunity cost threshold values (default: c(8000, 10000, 13000, 15000, 20000)).

title: Run DCEA Sensitivity Analysis — run_dcea_sensitivity
text: object class "dcea_sensitivity" elements: eta_profile Tibble: NHB SII change across eta range. one_way Tibble: results one-way sensitivity       parameters. tornado_data Data frame ready tornado plot. parameters List parameter ranges used.

title: Run DCEA Sensitivity Analysis — run_dcea_sensitivity
code: result <- run_aggregate_dcea(   icer = 25000, inc_qaly = 0.5, inc_cost = 12500,   population_size = 10000, wtp = 20000 ) sa <- run_dcea_sensitivity(result, params_to_vary = "eta") plot_dcea_tornado(sa) #> Warning: No tornado data available. Run with params_to_vary including 'wtp' or 'occ_threshold'.

title: Run Full-Form DCEA — run_full_dcea
text: Implements full-form DCEA subgroup-specific model parameters available (e.g., differential uptake, differential quality--life gains, differential survival socioeconomic group). Full-form DCEA appropriate : disease well-documented SES gradients clinical outcomes. Subgroup trial data real-world evidence available. NICE requires granular equity evidence (HST exceptional cases).

title: Run Full-Form DCEA — run_full_dcea
code: run_full_dcea(   subgroup_cea_results,   baseline_health,   wtp = 20000,   opportunity_cost_threshold = 13000,   uptake_by_group = NULL,   adherence_by_group = NULL,   comorbidity_adjustment = FALSE,   psa_iterations = NULL )

title: Run Full-Form DCEA — run_full_dcea
text: subgroup_cea_results Data frame one row per equity subgroup. Required columns: group (integer), group_label (character), inc_qaly (numeric), inc_cost (numeric), pop_share (numeric, sums 1). baseline_health Tibble get_baseline_health. wtp Willingness--pay threshold GBP/QALY (default: 20000). opportunity_cost_threshold Cost per QALY displaced care (default: 13000). uptake_by_group Optional named numeric vector uptake rates (0-1) per group. NULL, assumes equal uptake across groups. adherence_by_group Optional named numeric vector adherence rates (0-1) per group. Applied multiplier inc_qaly. comorbidity_adjustment Logical. TRUE, applies SES-based comorbidity adjustment QoL gains (experimental; default: FALSE). psa_iterations Optional integer. Number PSA iterations. provided, returns probabilistic NHB distribution group.

title: Run Full-Form DCEA — run_full_dcea
text: object class "full_dcea" elements analogous   run_aggregate_dcea plus subgroup-level detail.

title: Run Full-Form DCEA — run_full_dcea
code: baseline <- get_baseline_health("england", "imd_quintile") subgroup_data <- tibble::tibble(   group       = 1:5,   group_label = paste("IMD Q", 1:5),   inc_qaly    = c(0.30, 0.38, 0.45, 0.52, 0.58),   inc_cost    = c(12000, 11500, 11000, 10500, 10000),   pop_share   = rep(0.2, 5) ) result <- run_full_dcea(subgroup_data, baseline) summary(result) #> == Full-Form DCEA Result == #>   Net Health Benefit (equity-weighted): -0.1040 QALYs #>   SII change: 0.5423 #>   Decision:   Lose-Lose (efficiency loss + equity loss) #>  #> -- Per-group results -- #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>   group_label inc_qaly_adj      nhb baseline_hale post_hale #>   <chr>              <dbl>    <dbl>         <dbl>     <dbl> #> 1 IMD Q 1             0.3  -0.3              52.1      51.5 #> 2 IMD Q 2             0.38 -0.195            56.3      55.8 #> 3 IMD Q 3             0.45 -0.1              59.8      59.4 #> 4 IMD Q 4             0.52 -0.00500          63.2      62.9 #> 5 IMD Q 5             0.58  0.0800           66.8      66.6

text: run_aggregate_dcea() — aggregate DCEA following Love-Koh et al. (2019) run_full_dcea() — full-form DCEA subgroup-specific parameters calc_sii() / calc_rii() — Slope Relative Index Inequality calc_concentration_index() — standard, Erreygers, Wagstaff variants calc_atkinson_index() — Atkinson inequality index calc_gini() — Gini coefficient health distributions calc_all_inequality_indices() — calculate indices one call calc_ede() / calc_ede_profile() — Equally Distributed Equivalent health calc_social_welfare() — social welfare function analysis calc_equity_weights() / calc_equity_weighted_nhb() — equity weighting get_baseline_health() — preloaded baseline HALE data (England, Canada, ) merge_cea_with_baseline() — merge CEA output baseline distributions plot_equity_impact_plane() — equity-efficiency impact plane visualisation plot_lorenz_curve() — Lorenz Generalised Lorenz curves plot_ede_profile() — EDE profile across eta range plot_inequality_staircase() — causal pathway staircase visualisation run_dcea_sensitivity() — sensitivity analysis key DCEA parameters plot_dcea_tornado() — tornado diagram sensitivity results generate_nice_table() — NICE-formatted submission tables export_dcea_excel() — Excel export NICE submission format generate_dcea_report() — full HTML/Word/PDF DCEA reports launch_dcea_app() — interactive Shiny application
